sources.exact_document_title: Practice Guidelines for the Prevention, Detection, and Management of Respiratory Depression Associated with Neuraxial Opioid Administration
sources.population: Patients receiving neuraxial opioids, with patient-specific respiratory-risk qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: Periprocedural and postprocedural neuraxial opioid care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for focused risk history and documented ventilation, oxygenation, and consciousness monitoring. Opioid selection, dose, monitoring frequency, and treatment are not mapped.

exact_sections.heading: Focused history and physical examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting sleep apnea, comorbidity, medicines, previous response, and other respiratory-depression risk factors.

exact_sections.heading: Monitoring for respiratory depression
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documented ventilation, oxygenation, and level-of-consciousness assessment without specifying dose or generating treatment.

archive.title: Practice Guidelines for the Prevention, Detection, and Management of Respiratory Depression Associated with Neuraxial Opioid Administration
archive.population: Patients receiving neuraxial opioids, with patient-specific respiratory-risk qualifiers.
archive.setting: Periprocedural and postprocedural neuraxial opioid care.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Focused history and physical examination
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting sleep apnea, comorbidity, medicines, previous response, and other respiratory-depression risk factors.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Monitoring for respiratory depression
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documented ventilation, oxygenation, and level-of-consciousness assessment without specifying dose or generating treatment.

sources.exact_document_title: Practice Guidelines for the Perioperative Management of Patients with Obstructive Sleep Apnea
sources.population: Adult and pediatric patients older than 1 year with suspected or established obstructive sleep apnea in perioperative settings.
sources.clinical_setting: Preoperative, intraoperative, and postoperative anesthesia care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for record review, patient or family interview, airway examination, prior airway difficulty, OSA symptoms and studies, severity, procedure, and opioid-related risk context. Treatment and disposition recommendations are not mapped.

exact_sections.heading: Preoperative evaluation for suspected or established OSA
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports record review, patient or family interview, airway examination, prior airway difficulty, OSA symptoms, sleep-study results, and severity documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Determining perioperative risk from OSA
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting OSA severity, procedure invasiveness, postoperative analgesic context, and clinician-assessed perioperative risk without an automatic disposition or treatment.

archive.title: Practice Guidelines for the Perioperative Management of Patients with Obstructive Sleep Apnea
archive.population: Adult and pediatric patients older than 1 year with suspected or established obstructive sleep apnea in perioperative settings.
archive.setting: Preoperative, intraoperative, and postoperative anesthesia care.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Preoperative evaluation for suspected or established OSA
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports record review, patient or family interview, airway examination, prior airway difficulty, OSA symptoms, sleep-study results, and severity documentation.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Determining perioperative risk from OSA
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting OSA severity, procedure invasiveness, postoperative analgesic context, and clinician-assessed perioperative risk without an automatic disposition or treatment.

sources.population: Patients who receive general anesthesia, regional anesthesia, or monitored anesthesia care.
sources.clinical_setting: PACU or equivalent postanesthesia recovery location.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for transfer, arrival evaluation, handover, recovery monitoring and records, discharge criteria, and qualified discharge responsibility. It does not generate discharge or treatment decisions.

exact_sections.heading: Standards I–II — PACU transfer, arrival evaluation, and handover
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports transport with appropriate monitoring and support, arrival re-evaluation, verbal report, and documented transfer status.

exact_sections.heading: Standard III — continual evaluation and recovery record
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documented oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain or other recovery observations, and use of a recovery score where appropriate.

exact_sections.heading: Standards IV–V — discharge responsibility and criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports qualified discharge responsibility and institution-approved discharge criteria as recorded decisions, not automatic clearance.

archive.title: Standards for Postanesthesia Care
archive.population: Patients who receive general anesthesia, regional anesthesia, or monitored anesthesia care.
archive.setting: PACU or equivalent postanesthesia recovery location.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Standards I–II — PACU transfer, arrival evaluation, and handover
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports transport with appropriate monitoring and support, arrival re-evaluation, verbal report, and documented transfer status.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Standard III — continual evaluation and recovery record
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documented oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain or other recovery observations, and use of a recovery score where appropriate.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Standards IV–V — discharge responsibility and criteria
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports qualified discharge responsibility and institution-approved discharge criteria as recorded decisions, not automatic clearance.

sources.population: Patients recovering from anesthesia or sedation, with clinical and procedural qualifications.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for periodic recovery assessment domains including respiratory and cardiovascular status, mental status, temperature, pain, nausea or vomiting, hydration, urine output, drainage, and bleeding. Treatment recommendations are not mapped; recency is a blocker.

exact_sections.heading: Patient assessment and monitoring during recovery
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports periodic documented respiratory, cardiovascular, neurologic or mental, temperature, pain, nausea or vomiting, hydration, urine-output, drainage, and bleeding observations.

exact_sections.heading: Discharge assessment and documentation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment and documentation of discharge readiness and responsible discharge processes without automatic discharge.
